skills.description: Can define geography as the study of spatial patterns on Earth's surface
Can explain the difference between physical geography and human geography
Can identify what reference maps show (boundaries, roads, features)
Can identify what thematic maps show (specific data patterns)
Can identify choropleth maps (shading shows data by area)
Can identify dot distribution maps (dots show density)
Can identify graduated symbol maps (symbol size = quantity)
Can identify isoline maps (lines connect equal values)
Can identify cartograms (distorted areas represent data)
Can define absolute location using latitude and longitude
Can define relative location (position compared to other places)
Can explain absolute vs. relative distance
Can explain absolute vs. relative direction
Can identify CLUSTERED spatial patterns on maps
Can identify DISPERSED spatial patterns on maps
Can identify LINEAR spatial patterns on maps
Can define map scale (ratio of map distance to real distance)
Can explain large-scale vs. small-scale maps
Can explain how map projections distort shape, area, distance, or direction
Can identify Mercator projection (preserves shape, distorts area)
Can identify equal-area projections (preserves area, distorts shape)
Can define GIS (computer system for spatial data)
Can define GPS (satellite-based location system)
Can define remote sensing (data from satellites/aircraft)
Can explain quantitative vs. qualitative geographic data
Can identify fieldwork methods (interviews, surveys, sketches)
Can explain how geospatial data influences government policy
Can explain how businesses use geospatial data
Can define SPACE (physical area between points)
Can define PLACE (specific location with unique characteristics)
Can define SITE (internal characteristics of a place)
Can define SITUATION (external location relative to other places)
Can define DISTANCE DECAY (less interaction with more distance)
Can define TIME-SPACE COMPRESSION (technology reduces travel time)
Can define SPATIAL INTERACTION (flow of people, goods, ideas)
Can define PATTERN (arrangement of features in space)
Can define SUSTAINABILITY (meeting present needs without harming future)
Can explain HUMAN-ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
Can define natural resources
Can explain renewable vs. nonrenewable resources
Can explain ENVIRONMENTAL DETERMINISM (environment controls humans)
Can explain POSSIBILISM (humans adapt to/modify environment)
Can distinguish local, national, regional, and global scales
Can explain how patterns differ at different scales
Can define FORMAL REGIONS (uniform characteristics)
Can define FUNCTIONAL REGIONS (organized around a central point)
Can define PERCEPTUAL/VERNACULAR REGIONS (based on perception)
Can explain how regions are constructed by geographers
Can explain how regional boundaries can overlap and change
Can explain that population is unevenly distributed on Earth
Can explain PHYSICAL factors affecting population distribution (climate, terrain, water)
Can explain HUMAN factors affecting population distribution (economy, history)
Can define ARITHMETIC DENSITY (people ÷ total land)
Can define PHYSIOLOGICAL DENSITY (people ÷ arable land)
Can define AGRICULTURAL DENSITY (farmers ÷ arable land)
Can explain what different density measures reveal
Can define CARRYING CAPACITY (max population an area can support)
Can identify major population concentrations (East Asia, South Asia, Europe)
Can read and interpret POPULATION PYRAMIDS
Can identify EXPANSIVE pyramids (wide base = high birth rate)
Can identify CONSTRICTIVE pyramids (narrow base = low birth rate)
Can identify STATIONARY pyramids (stable growth)
Can define DEPENDENCY RATIO (dependents ÷ working-age)
Can explain how sex ratios vary due to culture and migration
Can define CRUDE BIRTH RATE (births per 1000 people)
Can define CRUDE DEATH RATE (deaths per 1000 people)
Can calculate RATE OF NATURAL INCREASE (CBR - CDR)
Can define TOTAL FERTILITY RATE (avg children per woman)
Can define INFANT MORTALITY RATE (infant deaths per 1000 births)
Can define LIFE EXPECTANCY
Can calculate DOUBLING TIME (≈70 ÷ growth rate)
Can describe the DEMOGRAPHIC TRANSITION MODEL (DTM)
Can identify DTM Stage 1 (high birth & death, low growth)
Can identify DTM Stage 2 (high birth, falling death, HIGH growth)
Can identify DTM Stage 3 (falling birth, low death, moderate growth)
Can identify DTM Stage 4 (low birth & death, very low growth)
Can identify DTM Stage 5 (birth below death, population decline)
Can explain what causes the Stage 1→2 transition (healthcare)
Can explain what causes the Stage 2→3 transition (urbanization)
Can link the DTM to industrialization and development
Can describe the EPIDEMIOLOGIC TRANSITION MODEL
Can identify ETM stages (pestilence → degenerative → reemergence)
Can explain MALTHUSIAN THEORY (population outgrows food)
Can explain NEO-MALTHUSIAN concerns
Can explain critiques of Malthus (technology, DTM)
Can explain BOSERUP THEORY (population drives innovation)
Can identify PRONATALIST policies (encourage births)
Can identify ANTINATALIST policies (reduce births)
Can identify China's one-child policy as antinatalist
Can explain how women's education reduces fertility
Can explain how healthcare access affects population
Can identify challenges of AGING POPULATIONS
Can explain how aging increases dependency ratios
Can explain government responses to aging (immigration, retirement age)
Can define MIGRATION (permanent move to new location)
Can explain IMMIGRATION vs. EMIGRATION
Can define NET MIGRATION (immigration - emigration)
Can explain PUSH vs. PULL factors
Can identify ECONOMIC push-pull factors
Can identify POLITICAL push-pull factors
Can identify ENVIRONMENTAL push-pull factors
Can identify DEMOGRAPHIC push-pull factors
Can explain VOLUNTARY vs. FORCED migration
Can define REFUGEES (flee due to persecution/conflict)
Can define INTERNALLY DISPLACED PERSONS (IDPs)
Can define ASYLUM SEEKERS
Can explain RAVENSTEIN'S LAWS of migration
Can explain that long-distance migrants go to major cities
Can explain STEP MIGRATION (moving in stages)
Can explain the GRAVITY MODEL for migration
Can define INTERVENING OBSTACLES (barriers to migration)
Can define INTERVENING OPPORTUNITIES (closer alternatives)
Can define CHAIN MIGRATION (family/friend networks)
Can define GUEST WORKERS (temporary labor migrants)
Can define TRANSHUMANCE (seasonal livestock movement)
Can define TRANSNATIONAL MIGRATION (ties to both places)
Can define REMITTANCES (money sent home by migrants)
Can explain effects of emigration (brain drain, labor loss)
Can explain effects of immigration (diversity, labor supply)
Can explain how migration changes demographics
Can define DIASPORA (dispersed people from homeland)
Can define CULTURE (shared practices, attitudes, behaviors)
Can define CULTURAL TRAITS (individual elements like food, clothing)
Can define CULTURAL COMPLEXES (related traits grouped together)
Can define CULTURAL HEARTHS (origin areas of culture)
Can identify early hearths (Mesopotamia, Nile, Indus, etc.)
Can explain FOLK CULTURE vs. POPULAR CULTURE
Can explain that folk culture is clustered
Can explain that popular culture spreads widely/quickly
Can define CULTURAL LANDSCAPE (human impact on landscape)
Can identify examples of cultural landscapes
Can explain how religion shapes cultural landscapes
Can explain SEQUENT OCCUPANCE (successive cultural imprints)
Can define CULTURAL DIFFUSION (spread of culture)
Can define RELOCATION DIFFUSION (spread through movement)
Can define EXPANSION DIFFUSION (spreads outward from source)
Can define HIERARCHICAL DIFFUSION (spreads to major places first)
Can define CONTAGIOUS DIFFUSION (rapid, like a disease)
Can define STIMULUS DIFFUSION (idea spreads, not exact trait)
Can explain how colonialism spread European culture
Can explain how trade routes spread culture (Silk Road)
Can explain how the internet accelerates diffusion
Can explain how transportation increases diffusion
Can define ACCULTURATION (adopting some traits of dominant culture)
Can define ASSIMILATION (complete integration into dominant culture)
Can define SYNCRETISM (blending of cultures/religions)
Can define MULTICULTURALISM (coexisting diverse cultures)
Can classify languages into families, branches, groups
Can identify Indo-European as most spoken language family
Can identify other major language families
Can define LINGUA FRANCA (common language between groups)
Can identify English as a global lingua franca
Can define PIDGIN languages (simplified communication)
Can define CREOLE languages (pidgins become native)
Can define DIALECTS (regional language variations)
Can define ISOGLOSSES (dialect boundaries)
Can explain how language shapes identity
Can explain how languages become endangered/extinct
Can explain UNIVERSALIZING vs. ETHNIC religions
Can identify Christianity, Islam, Buddhism as universalizing
Can identify Hinduism and Judaism as ethnic religions
Can identify hearths of major world religions
Can explain how universalizing religions spread
Can explain why ethnic religions stay concentrated
Can identify SYNCRETIC religions (blended traditions)
Can identify sacred sites and pilgrimage destinations
Can explain how religion shapes cultural landscapes
Can define ETHNOCENTRISM (judging others by own standards)
Can define CULTURAL RELATIVISM (evaluate by own standards)
Can explain CULTURAL CONVERGENCE (globalization spreads culture)
Can explain CULTURAL DIVERGENCE (groups resist globalization)
Can define PLACELESSNESS (loss of unique place identity)
Can explain how popular culture threatens folk culture
Can explain media's role in cultural change
Can explain how culture affects gender roles
Can define NATIVISM (protecting native-born over immigrants)
Can define STATE (territory, population, government, sovereignty)
Can define NATION (group with shared cultural identity)
Can define NATION-STATE (state = one nation)
Can identify examples of nation-states (Japan, Iceland)
Can define MULTINATIONAL STATE (state with multiple nations)
Can define MULTISTATE NATION (nation across multiple states)
Can define STATELESS NATION (nation without own state)
Can identify Kurds as a stateless nation
Can define SOVEREIGNTY (supreme authority over territory)
Can explain SELF-DETERMINATION (right to choose government)
Can explain how COLONIALISM created modern boundaries
Can explain DECOLONIZATION in the 20th century
Can define NEOCOLONIALISM (economic control by former powers)
Can define POLITICAL BOUNDARY
Can define ANTECEDENT boundaries (drawn before settlement)
Can define SUBSEQUENT boundaries (drawn after settlement)
Can define SUPERIMPOSED boundaries (forced by outside powers)
Can define RELIC boundaries (no longer function)
Can explain NATURAL vs. GEOMETRIC boundaries
Can explain boundary processes (define, delimit, demarcate)
Can identify DEFINITIONAL disputes (treaty language)
Can identify LOCATIONAL disputes (where boundary goes)
Can identify OPERATIONAL disputes (how boundary functions)
Can identify ALLOCATIONAL disputes (resource distribution)
Can explain UNCLOS and Exclusive Economic Zones
Can define TERRITORIAL WATERS (12 nautical miles)
Can define EEZ (200 nautical miles for resources)
Can explain GERRYMANDERING
Can explain PACKING vs. CRACKING in gerrymandering
Can explain REDISTRICTING (redraw districts after census)
Can explain REAPPORTIONMENT (redistribute legislative seats)
Can define FEDERAL state (power divided between levels)
Can define UNITARY state (power concentrated in central gov)
Can compare federal and unitary systems
Can identify characteristics of DEMOCRACIES
Can identify characteristics of AUTHORITARIAN systems
Can define DEVOLUTION (power transfers to local government)
Can explain physical causes of devolution (distance, barriers)
Can explain ETHNIC SEPARATISM as cause of devolution
Can explain ECONOMIC INEQUALITY as cause of devolution
Can identify devolution examples (Scotland, Catalonia, Quebec)
Can define CENTRIPETAL forces (unify a state)
Can define CENTRIFUGAL forces (divide a state)
Can identify centripetal examples (nationalism, infrastructure)
Can identify centrifugal examples (ethnic conflict, inequality)
Can define IRREDENTISM (claim territory based on ethnicity)
Can define TERRORISM (nonstate violence for political goals)
Can explain SUPRANATIONAL organizations (UN, EU, NATO)
Can explain how supranational orgs affect sovereignty
Can define AUTONOMOUS/SEMI-AUTONOMOUS regions
Can identify state shapes (compact, elongated, fragmented, etc.)
Can explain how state shape affects governance
Can define TERRITORIALITY (attempt to control area)
Can explain HEARTLAND THEORY (interior Eurasia = power)
Can explain RIMLAND THEORY (coastal Eurasia = power)
Can define CHOKE POINTS (strategic narrow waterways)
Can define BUFFER STATES (reduce conflict between powers)
Can define SHATTERBELT regions (unstable between powers)
Can identify ENCLAVES (territory surrounded by another state)
Can identify EXCLAVES (separated from main territory)
Can define FRONTIER (zone of no clear control)
Can define AGRICULTURE
Can explain the FIRST AGRICULTURAL REVOLUTION (Neolithic)
Can identify plant/animal domestication in First Ag Rev
Can identify early agricultural hearths (Fertile Crescent, etc.)
Can explain how agriculture diffused from hearths

Can identify NEGATIVE effects of Green Revolution
Can explain SUBSISTENCE vs. COMMERCIAL agriculture

Can define PASTORAL NOMADISM

Can explain RANCHING
Can explain MEDITERRANEAN agriculture
Can explain how climate/terrain/soil affect agriculture

Can explain how transportation changes Von Thünen
Can explain BOSERUP THEORY (population drives ag innovation)

Can explain agricultural dependency in developing countries
Can explain WTO, trade agreements, and agricultural subsidies

Can define FOOD DESERTS

Can explain DESERTIFICATION from agriculture

Can explain core-periphery in global food system

Can explain women's role in agriculture

Can identify LONG LOT survey system

Can explain how land surveys influence settlement patterns
